title: Conformal Prediction for Macroeconomic Forecasting
description: Distribution-free prediction intervals across four FRED series and three macroeconomic regimes.

references.citation: Angelopoulos, A. N., & Bates, S. (2023). Conformal Prediction: A Gentle Introduction. Foundations and Trends in Machine Learning, 16(4), 494–591.
Cordier, T., et al. (2023). MAPIE: an open-source library for distribution-free uncertainty quantification. arXiv:2207.12274.
Federal Reserve Bank of St. Louis. FRED Economic Data.
National Bureau of Economic Research. US Business Cycle Expansions and Contractions.

title: California IOU General Rate Case Analysis
description: Reconstructed CPUC staff's Phase 1 GRC baseline methodology for PG&E, SCE, and SDG&E — identifying PG&E's O&M at 46% above the California IOU peer average using public FERC Form 1 and EIA-861 data.

references.citation: FERC Form 1 data accessed via PUDL Project (Catalyst Cooperative).
EIA Form 861 Annual Electric Power Industry Report, U.S. Energy Information Administration.
CPUC General Rate Case process overview.
FERC Uniform System of Accounts for Public Utilities (18 C.F.R. Part 101).

title: California Solar Adoption & Grid Evolution
description: Traced how 1.8M+ residential solar installations reshaped California's duck curve and drove net-load ramp magnitude up ~600 MW/hour between 2015 and 2023, using CEC DGStats and CAISO OASIS hourly signals.

references.citation: California Energy Commission. Distributed Generation Statistics (DGStats).
California ISO. OASIS (Open Access Same-time Information System) hourly demand and generation data.
U.S. Census Bureau. TIGER/Line Shapefiles — ZIP Code Tabulation Areas.
CPUC. Net Energy Metering (NEM 2.0) and Net Billing Tariff (NBT) policy transition.

title: Neon Econometrics Showcase
description: A stylized econometric analysis project leveraging AI-assisted tooling for compelling data storytelling and neon aesthetics.

title: The Data Observatory
description: An experimental portfolio concept built around the metaphor of an observatory — watching data like fungi watch the forest.
